meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Fix Slow Development Speed for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop manual coding bottlenecks. Learn to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-end freelance projects at 10x the speed.
content.answer_block: To eliminate development bottlenecks, integrate Antigravity for rapid UI generation and leverage Claude Code for autonomous architectural implementation. By shifting from manual coding to AI-orchestrated design, you transform your workflow from a linear task-based process into a high-leverage system, allowing you to ship production-ready features in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
The late-night grind is a badge of honor for the amateur, but for the professional, it is a sign of a broken process. You are stuck in the 'pixel-pushing trap,' where your billable hours are consumed by repetitive boilerplate code and layout adjustments rather than high-value architectural decisions. The deadline is looming, the client is restless, and your velocity is stalled by the friction of manual implementation.

This is where the paradigm shifts. By moving from manual coding to AI-assisted design, you reclaim your agency as a builder. We aren't just writing code; we are orchestrating systems. Using the synergy of Antigravity's visual design capabilities and Claude Code’s command-line precision, you can bridge the gap between abstract requirements and deployed reality at unprecedented speeds.

steps.title: Define UI Architecture in Antigravity
steps.description: Translate your client's wireframes into functional design tokens using Antigravity to establish a consistent component library before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive dashboard layout system using Tailwind CSS. Include a sidebar navigation, a top-level statistics header, and a main data table component. Use semantic HTML5 and ensure all components are modular.

steps.title: Generate Component Schema
steps.description: Convert design tokens into structured JSON schemas that Claude Code can parse to build the underlying logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a JSON schema for the dashboard component props, including types for user authentication state, data fetching hooks, and layout configuration variables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Convert the Antigravity schema into a typed TypeScript interface file in /src/types/dashboard.ts"

steps.title: Implement State Management
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold your global state logic based on the design requirements established in step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define the state management requirements for the dashboard. Need persistent auth, theme toggling, and real-time data subscription handles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Implement Zustand store for auth and theme management based on the provided schema"

steps.title: Rapid Logic Injection
steps.description: Automate the connection between the UI components and your API endpoints using Claude Code's context-aware file editing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create data-fetching hooks for the dashboard table that handle loading states, error boundaries, and pagination.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Create react-query hooks for the dashboard table component using axios"

steps.title: Regression and Polish
steps.description: Perform an automated code review to ensure the AI-generated output adheres to your personal coding standards and performance benchmarks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase for potential performance bottlenecks in the dashboard rendering and suggest optimizations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Audit the current build for performance optimizations and linting errors"

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'golden' prompt library in a Notion doc so your Antigravity outputs remain consistent across different client projects.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests *before* the implementation code to ensure the AI's logic is constrained by your specific requirements.
Force the AI to use 'Atomic Commits' by including a command in your Claude instructions to commit after every individual component completion.
Keep your design tokens in a separate repository; use Claude Code to fetch them as a remote dependency for every new project to ensure brand consistency.
When facing a complex bug, use Claude Code to explain the trace log before asking for a fix; it forces the model to reason through the stack.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, begin building your own boilerplate repository that integrates these AI tools, allowing you to bootstrap future projects in under 30 minutes.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the code and start mastering the architecture. This is the new standard for the high-performing freelancer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
